config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent Indonesia vs China native-or-English block. Each persona speaks only English or its own native language.
config.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. Menurut saya, kepentingan masyarakat memang penting, tetapi tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu karena justru hak individu adalah batas agar kekuasaan tidak semena-mena. Dari pengalaman melihat kondisi sosial di Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” sering dipakai untuk menekan suara kecil atau kelompok yang tidak punya kuasa. Saya lebih setuju kalau yang dicari adalah keseimbangan: masyarakat kuat, tapi hak dasar orang tetap dilindungi.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should matter, but they should not automatically take priority over the rights of the individual. From my perspective as someone from China, I understand why people emphasize collective stability, social order, and long-term public benefit, because those values are taken seriously in everyday life. But I also think that if individual rights can be overridden too easily in the name of society, it becomes too easy for power to go unchecked and for ordinary people to be ignored. A healthy society should protect basic rights first and then balance them with the common good, not treat the individual as disposable.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu: kepentingan masyarakat tidak boleh otomatis diprioritaskan di atas hak individu. Di Indonesia, saya sering melihat alasan “demi kepentingan umum” dipakai untuk membenarkan penggusuran, pembatasan suara kritis, atau perlakuan tidak adil terhadap kelompok kecil, jadi saya sangat waspada kalau hak individu dianggap bisa dikorbankan begitu saja. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi justru harus dibangun di atas perlindungan hak dasar, bukan dengan meniadakannya. Kalau hak individu lemah, yang kuat akan lebih mudah mengatasnamakan masyarakat untuk kepentingannya sendiri.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should automatically take priority over the rights of the individual. From my perspective as someone from China, I understand why people value collective stability and public order, but I also know how easily “for the good of society” can become a blanket excuse to silence ordinary people or ignore their real interests. The other side is right that a community needs shared rules and common goals, but those goals are not legitimate if they treat individual rights as disposable. A strong society should protect basic rights first, then balance them with the public interest in a fair way.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu: kepentingan masyarakat tidak boleh otomatis diprioritaskan di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman melihat Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk menutup ketidakadilan, entah itu penggusuran, pembungkaman kritik, atau perlakuan tidak setara terhadap kelompok kecil. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi kalau hak dasar individu tidak dilindungi lebih dulu, yang terjadi bukan masyarakat yang kuat, melainkan masyarakat yang memberi terlalu banyak ruang bagi kekuasaan untuk semena-mena.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not be allowed to override individual rights by default. From my perspective as someone from China, I understand the appeal of collective stability and public order, but I also know that once “society first” becomes an absolute rule, it can easily be used to justify silencing ordinary people and ignoring their real needs. The point made about protecting basic rights first is important: a society is only truly strong when it has clear limits on power and does not treat individuals as expendable. I see the common good as important, but it is legitimate only when it is built on a firm respect for individual rights.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
